cross_references.rule: at least 2 peers must come from prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates (enforcement via grounding check)

grounding_checks.description: subject_scores.roic_persistence_0_to_5 must be within 1 point of agent_bundle.quality_scores.roic_persistence
subject_scores.balance_sheet_0_to_5 must be within 1 point of agent_bundle.four_ms.balance_sheet * 5
subject_scores.management_0_to_5 must be within 1 point of agent_bundle.four_ms.management * 5
subject_scores.pricing_power_0_to_5 must be within 1 point of agent_bundle.quality_scores.pricing_power_score * 5
All peer names must be distinct — no peer company should appear twice
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: duplicate peer names found in output.peers[].name

grounding_checks.description: The subject company ticker or name must not appear in the peers list
At least 2 of the named peers must appear in prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: count of output.peers[].name values that match any entry in prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates < 2

grounding_checks.description: subject_scores.commentary must be at least 100 characters
Each peer commentary must be at least 100 characters
Subject ROIC persistence should not be significantly below peer average
Subject balance sheet should not be significantly below peer average

peer_validity.prompt: Are all peers real, named public companies in the same competitive set as the subject? Do at least 2-3 come from prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates? Is the subject company absent from the peer list?
peer_validity.score_5: All 3+ peers are real, named competitors in the same industry; 2+ from industry sprint; no hallucinations; subject absent
peer_validity.score_3: 2-3 real competitors; 1 from industry sprint; no obvious hallucinations
peer_validity.score_1: Hallucinated, tangential, or non-existent companies; or subject appears in peer list

scoring_calibration.prompt: Are subject_scores anchored to agent_bundle data (roic_persistence, balance_sheet, management, pricing_power)? Are peer scores internally consistent (the more moated company scores higher on roic_persistence; stronger balance sheet scores higher on balance_sheet_strength)?
scoring_calibration.score_5: Subject scores match agent_bundle within tolerance; peer rankings are internally consistent and defensible
scoring_calibration.score_3: Subject scores mostly aligned; 1-2 peer scores slightly miscalibrated but within range
scoring_calibration.score_1: Subject scores wildly off from agent_bundle; peer scores appear random or inverted

comparative_insight.prompt: Does each peer's commentary identify 1-2 decisive competitive differences vs the subject? Does the scorecard reveal where the subject leads and where it lags — not just a list of scores?
comparative_insight.score_5: Each peer has specific comparative commentary citing actual competitive advantages/disadvantages; subject positioning is explicit
comparative_insight.score_3: Most peers have substantive commentary; 1 is generic; subject positioning partially clear
comparative_insight.score_1: Generic commentary that could apply to any company; no specific competitive differentiation; unclear why scores differ

red_flags.prompt: Any signs of score inflation (everyone 4-5), score deflation (everyone 1-2), generic commentary, or subject scores that misrepresent agent_bundle data?
red_flags.score_5: Score distribution is realistic (not all 4-5); commentary is specific; no inflation or deflation pattern
red_flags.score_3: Minor scoring narrowness (e.g., 3-4 range) or 1 generic comment; grounding clear
red_flags.score_1: Widespread inflation, deflation, or all commentary is generic placeholder text

framework_focus: Compare the subject company against 3+ named peers from prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates on 7 QVG-aligned criteria: pricing_power, recurrence, roic_persistence, balance_sheet, management, industry_positioning, valuation_flex (0-5 each). Include subject_scores using the same 7 criteria — anchor subject's roic_persistence, balance_sheet, management, and pricing_power scores to agent_bundle data. Peer scores are qualitative estimates from general knowledge; this data limitation must be acknowledged. The comparison reveals relative positioning: where the subject leads peers (potential moat) and where it lags (risk or improvement area). Avoid score inflation — real competitive differentiation means scores should vary meaningfully across the 7 dimensions.
notes: 06_peers extends the spec schema (qualitative-analysis-v2.md §8.6) by adding subject_scores alongside the peer array. This is intentional: the thesis sprint needs subject vs peer comparison in a single structured output. Subject scores for roic_persistence, balance_sheet, management, and pricing_power are grounded in agent_bundle and verified by hard grounding checks (within ±1 tolerance). Peer scores are qualitative estimates — no peer quantitative data is available in agent_bundle. Item 1 text is not used in this sprint (data_validator.py item1_text: false for 06_peers). Peer candidates must come from prior_outputs.03_industry.peer_candidates to prevent hallucination.
